body:
Bead: ddx-8f1ac866 — Fizeau CONTRACT-003 extension for ExcludeProvider + ProviderID/Class/ActualPower + review cost

Outcome: no_changes — bead is structurally not executable from this DDx execute-bead worktree.

Why no commit was produced
==========================

The acceptance contract says:

  AC1. CONTRACT-003 update merged upstream.
  AC2. ddx imports the new agent module via tagged release (NEVER go.mod replace).
  AC3. Schema changes documented.

AC1 and AC2 require work that lives outside this repository:

- AC1 mutates `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau` (`~/Projects/agent`),
  a separate Git repository, and culminates in a merged PR / pushed tag
  on that repo's `master`. The DDx execute-bead worktree is rooted at
  `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8f1ac866-20260502T114201-218d37bb`
  with the constraint "Work only inside this execution worktree." Editing
  ~/Projects/agent, committing there, and pushing a `vX.Y.Z` tag to its
  remote are all outside that boundary and would also bypass DDx's
  per-attempt isolation guarantees.

- AC2 cannot be satisfied without AC1 first producing a tagged Fizeau
  release that exposes the new ExecuteRequest.ExcludeProvider field and
  the new ExecuteResponse / drain-result fields (ProviderID,
  ProviderClass, ActualPower, separated reviewer cost). The current
  imported version is `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28`; the
  newest tag at upstream is `v0.9.28` and inspection of `service.go`
  + `service_events.go` (DrainExecuteResult) shows none of the four
  new fields exist yet. Per durable user feedback ("Never use local
  replace directives"), a temporary go.mod replace pointing at a local
  Fizeau worktree is not an acceptable workaround.

- AC3 is a documentation deliverable that follows the schema change.
  Documenting fields that do not yet exist in CONTRACT-003 would
  invert the dependency direction.

Investigation done in this attempt
==================================

- Confirmed import path and version: `cli/go.mod` pins
  `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28`.
- Read the existing ExecuteRequest definition at
  `~/Projects/agent/service.go:577-665` and confirmed there is no
  ExcludeProvider field today.
- Confirmed Fizeau exposes a streaming Execute (`<-chan ServiceEvent`)
  rather than a request/response struct; the natural home for the new
  ProviderID / ProviderClass / ActualPower / separated review-cost
  fields is `DrainExecuteResult` in `service_events.go` (and/or the
  per-event metadata of the terminal `final` event), not a literal
  `ExecuteResponse` — the bead's "ExecuteResponse" wording is a
  contract-doc shorthand.
- Verified `~/Projects/agent` is a working clone on `master` with
  uncommitted, unrelated WIP — meaning even if cross-repo work were
  in scope, this attempt would risk entangling unrelated changes in
  any upstream commit/tag.

What a successful follow-up needs
=================================

This bead is properly executed in `~/Projects/agent`, not in DDx.
The follow-up should:

1. In `~/Projects/agent`, file an upstream bead (or use the parallel
   Fizeau queue) to:
   a. Add `ExcludeProvider []string` to `ServiceExecuteRequest` in
      `service.go` and the contract doc
      `docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md`.
      Wire it into the routing path so candidates whose provider
      identifier matches any entry are filtered before scoring (see
      `service_routing.go`). Define the matching domain explicitly
      (provider key vs. endpoint host vs. class).
   b. Extend the response surface — concretely `DrainExecuteResult`
      in `service_events.go` and the terminal `final` event payload —
      with `ProviderID string`, `ProviderClass string`, and
      `ActualPower int` (the power band the routing actually selected,
      not the requested MinPower/MaxPower window).
   c. Split reviewer cost out of the aggregate cost field so callers
      can attribute review spend separately (likely a new
      `ReviewerCost` / `ReviewerUSD` field on the drain result and a
      review-tagged usage event).
   d. Update CONTRACT-003 §"Public types" with the new fields and
      semantics. Add tests covering: ExcludeProvider filters the
      named provider out of the candidate set; the four new response
      fields are populated end-to-end through Execute → DrainExecute.
   e. Cut a release tag (proposed `v0.9.29`).

2. In DDx, after the upstream tag exists:
   a. `cd cli && go get github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau@v0.9.29 && go mod tidy`.
      No `replace` directives, even transiently.
   b. Re-run `cd cli && go test ./...` to confirm the bump compiles
      cleanly against existing call sites
      (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`,
      `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go`,
      `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`).
   c. Reference the new fields where the endpoint-first redesign needs
      them — but only as part of the dependent S10_3 / S10_5 / S10_6
      beads, not this bead.
   d. Cross-link the upstream PR/tag from
      `docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md` (or a
      new audit addendum) per AC3.

Recommended decomposition
=========================

This bead should be split, with the cross-repo dependency made explicit:

- ddx-8f1ac866-A (upstream-Fizeau): "CONTRACT-003 §ExecuteRequest:
  add ExcludeProvider field + filter wiring + tag release"
- ddx-8f1ac866-B (upstream-Fizeau): "CONTRACT-003 §ExecuteResponse /
  DrainExecuteResult: add ProviderID/ProviderClass/ActualPower +
  separate reviewer cost + tag release"
- ddx-8f1ac866-C (this repo): "Bump Fizeau to the new tag in
  cli/go.mod; refresh contract-003 audit with the new field table"

Children A and B are sized for one Fizeau attempt each. C is a
small DDx attempt blocked on A and B landing.

I did not file these children automatically: `ddx bead create` from
this worktree would write children into the DDx tracker, but the
upstream repository is the wrong tracker for A and B. The orchestrator
or a human should file A/B in the Fizeau bead queue (`~/Projects/agent`)
and re-scope this DDx bead down to C only.

End of rationale.
